context.scenario:
I F17 and this guy (we'll call him j) M17 became friends through the app WIZZ. He had messaged me first and I was super excited because he was an alternative guy with long hair and that's what my type mostly is. When we met he did call me pretty and I took it as a compliment. We exchanged our Instagram's and we talked. J was pretty dry through text and I asked him if we could call. He agreed and I thought we'd be able to bond, as soon as we got on the call he started having aggressive behavior which made me not like him very much. J would call me slow, stupid, and dumb which made me feel unliked as I do have BPD and I saw it as a form of rejection. J would continue on with making snarky mean comments and boast about w\*\*d. I wanted to play Roblox and asked him if he'd like to join me. He agreed again. He had made comments during this call that he was still in love with his ex who was a trans masc., so I wasn't going to pursue him.

(in general I hate making people upset so when asked to do something I don't want to do I agree and yes I am working on it in therapy.)

J starts making deals with me around the lines of "If I get past this stage before you, you have to send me \_\_\_." I agreed. I struggle badly with obby's so you can put two and two together. We played Roblox for a long time that night and I fell asleep while being on the phone with him. The next day he was a little more flirty which I have to admit is my fault I did engage in flirtatious conversations and didn't say "No" to any advances he made towards me. I too was still in love with my ex who had cheated on me 2 months before I met J. I used J as an escape and that was wrong of me, I do accept full responsibility. 

Around the time of Halloween, J was texting me while I was in my 2nd period class, he said "I love you" and I told him straight up that we weren't dating and it was way too soon to say things like this and that I couldn't reciprocate the words back to him which upset him for a few hours. We went back to how things were again after the incident.

 October 29th he finally asked me to be his girlfriend. I tried to pretend that I didn't hear him and that I had fallen asleep. I didn't want to discuss why I didn't want to be dating him and felt bad. He asked me a second time when I had "woken up" and I just said yes. I feel so bad about leading him onto this moment and I feel like I probably hurt him quite a bit. I decided to hang up an hour later and I blocked him. I had forgotten I had him added on another acc he had messaged me.

I still feel extremely bad that I led him on I know it wasn't an overreaction on his end and I'm not sure how to handle anything anymore because he texted my current boyfriend to call me names and slurs and insult my current boyfriend as well, I told my boyfriend everything and he supports me but I'm not sure if it's fair and if its okay because my boyfriend loves me and doesn't care what I did in the past. So AITA?
context.comment:
Sweet summer angel, hear me when I say this: WOMEN ARE NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR MEN'S FRAGILE FEELINGS.  You are a worthy, capable young lady who is in no way deserving of abusive treatment - verbal, physical, emotional, etc.  You have every right to cut someone out of your life without question, for treating you with disrespect.  As a mother, it is my deepest wish that I can instill confidence in my daughter, and that she will know her worth.  You should know your worth.  You are a valuable asset in this world, and are worth FAR more than this knobby kneed little shit who is trying to tear you down.  Are you really going to let a dude who can't even play Roblox tell you who you are?  NO MA'AM!  YOU KNOW WHO YOU ARE.  Do not let this fool, or any fool for that matter, trick you into thinking you are unworthy of them, when it is them who are unworthy of you.

NTA, obviously.  You are a bright little summer moon - illuminate the world with your gifts and don't let someone else dim your shine so that they can feel brighter.

context.scenario:
Trying to keep it short. But I am really confused right now.

I (33M)was planning for a nice date with my wife(40F, married for 7 years) today. Got her a present(nice necklace) and booked a table at s nice restaurant we like to go to. She mentioned wanting a new necklace and has been looking recently, so I got her one that would fit her description.

We are having a tough time at the moment trying to ger pregnant without succes and even had one unsuccesfull ivf. So not an easy time. 
I tried to cheer her up buy planning a nice date.

When I came home from work she was not ready to go out, we had to leave after 15mins so she started quickly.
I had the gift hidden in my coat and she did not know about it.
After a few minutes I suggested her to wear the new skirt she bought last week. Nothing crazy, not super short..
She starts to get mad at me. Tells me its not the weather for that, I tell her its fine and to just wear something she likes.
However she gets more angry by the minute. Starts yelling at me to just go ahead and meet a 20 year old who wears short skirts like I like them. I tell her that nonsense but she keeps getting angrier. She starts throwing insults at me, telling me that I had no chance with a younger girl anyway, asks me if I had looked in the mirror recently… ouch.
She tells me that if I want to leave for a younger girl, now is the time. I keep instisting that not what I am thinking about.

I try to explain that I just wanted to have a nice date, tell her I even got her a present… she ignores me.

Datenight is not happening, present just sitting on the kitchen table. I am getting ignored for the past 2 hours.

Am I really the asshole for suggesting her to wear the skirt she bought a few days ago??
context.comment: It's highly unlikely that it's about the skirt for her, my dude.  Something else must be troubling her, likely the struggles of the IVF journey.  Maybe she feels like she's failing you, herself, that life is unfair, anger that her body is betraying her, frustration, insecurity, etc.  Sometimes when life piles it on you, you just start throwing things back haphazardly at it, often at the ones you're closest to.  There's no excuse for her degrading the way you look though.  I'm sure if you said those same things there would be hell to pay.  NTA but again, this is probably a symptom of a deeper issue she's going through.  Hopefully you can get her to open up and also to apologize for how she treated you.

context.scenario:
My husband and I have been together for 17 years. We have had a stressful time of it recently and have been bickering. We have even discussed divorce. We have two young children and do not take that decision lightly and both of us have been hoping it’s a difficult time which will pass. 
My MIL stays with us for a week or so every now and then and her and I have always been relatively close. Recently though she has been unable to hear a word even slightly negative in regard to her son and has been passive aggressive with me. She’s a heavy drinker after 6pm which is when she usually says things she shouldn’t and then forgets the next day.
My husband and I are careful not to argue in front of our kids but had a little disagreement and she flew off the handle and screamed ‘YOU TWO NEED TO SPLIT UP!!!!’ infront of my children. My children have never heard anything like that and they were upset and thought this was basically an announcement. She spent the night telling my husband he is better off without me, to divorce me and sell our house. She has never spoken about me like this before. 
She then made a big show about it, cried her heart out and kept calling my kids over to comfort her. I asked her to leave in the morning and I don’t plan on having her back anytime soon.
She doesn’t regret what she has said at all. She says she is sorry she said it infront of the children but it’s been a long time coming. She stands firm on this.
The next day I told her to leave and I don’t want her staying in my house again. For starters, I don’t want to normalise this behaviour and I do feel since she has crossed this line it will happen again, especially with the amount she drinks. 
My husband is in an awkward position and I’m pretty sure he thinks I’m being dramatic by not letting her come back to stay as she usually does the next time she’s in the country. 
Am I the unreasonable one here?
context.comment:
"She then made a big show about it, cried her heart out and kept calling my kids over to comfort her."

Oh ffs.  I grew up in an environment like this and...it's fucking exhausting.  NTA - I love the irony of your MIL calling out your alleged toxicity to adults, while being toxic to children.  She's exposing children to adult problems while handling her own adult problems like a child.  Then expecting said children to comfort her as an adult??? About problems that aren't even hers?  So she's making YOUR problem hers AND the kids?  How lovely of her.  Good luck, OP.

context.scenario:
We are expecting and due Oct 31. 

My mother was going to throw her / us a baby shower because she wants to do this for us and she's excited as it's her first grandkid.  

In PA, around me at least, baby showers are traditionally easy going lunch gatherings of women who play games and eat food and get favors and have raffles, etc. 

Where she is from, in NY, baby showers are for men and women to get together and party all night and have DJs and dance floors and are just different in general I guess.  

We are on 1 income currently and we're initially not going to spend anything on the shower as it was being thrown for us.  

My Fiancé is understandly upset that none of her family will likely be able to attend, as they are scattered around other states and not nearby.  


At first she said she didn't want a baby shower, and then she decided that if she was going to have a baby shower it needed to be decorated extremely well as to be picturesque at every turn, and to have a balloon arch and special chair (like a peacock chair) to take pictures at and the dessert and food table should have nice platters to serve the desserts from whatever food we get. She wants every table to be decorated with the table cloth and center piece and decorations, which is understandable and I've been helping plan it so that it can be her party. We also bought balloon arches and we're going to add the balloons and we were going to rent a special chair and we were going to decorate as well as we could, with the decorations I've bought and anything else we can find on FB or Amazon.    

She did not want my mom to plan out any of the decorations at all because she is afraid of how it will look and has referenced what my mom was going to do as simply a baby shower pizza party.  She also has said before thet the baby shower was just for my mom, which is why we have taken over planning of it completely so that she feels like it is for my Fiancé / us.  

Last night we were talking about it and she said she felt overwhelmed by what we had left to do with certain things.  I tried to reassure her though I probably could have done a better job.  She was overwhelmed about making the balloon arch and getting a peacock chair and just putting other decorations together and getting the rest of the decorations.  While I was trying to reassure her, she said but it still isn't the baby shower I want, because her family won't be there and it isn't a giant house party like she says they do in NY.  

I was exasperated at this point and I told her she was being ungrateful, as she didn't even want a baby shower, then we took over creating one because of the perception of it not being decorated well enough if my mom threw it for us,  and after all of this planning to make it her own, it still isn't what she actually wants.  


 I understand I probably shouldn't have said it, AITA?
context.comment:
NTA but there are things you and your gf can do to minimize the stress.  Blow the balloons up the night before and bag them in an industrial trash bag.  Decorate the peacock chair as best as you can ahead of time, and try to pack it up delicately.  I recommend getting a large block of nice fabric from Joanne's and wrapping it with a ribbon.  Utilize cheap disposable fringe skirts for the table, as well as disposable tablecloths and streamers.  If you have a dry erase board, use that to decorate your welcome to your guests.  You can also use it to play party games with the guests.  Create a Spotify playlist that you can fire up for music.  You can get cheap beverage containers from the dollar store and mix your own non-alcoholic beverages, or have a tea station if you want to be fancier.  

I've also used fringe to decorate the walls, and taped balloons directly to the wall for photography purposes.  Packing tape works super well for this, and the scattered balloons make the 3d effect look classier.  I am an event planner for large scale events, and I am always looking to make a big impact for the best budget.  NTA, it sounds like you want the moment to be special but are just not sure how to execute it.  Party planning is a lot, and if you aren't used to it, it can easily be overwhelming.

context.scenario:
My (43F) son (14M) is in the same class as this girl, let’s call her Maya. I’ve known Maya since they were little and she’s always been one of those really high-achieving kids. Her mom is a professor in electrical engineering and very focused on academics. Maya has been doing extra activities since she was a kid, coding, math competitions, spelling bees, all of it.

She’s clearly smart, no doubt, but lately I’ve just started feeling a little sad for her. I’ve noticed she also loves dressing up, makeup, all the girly stuff. She’s always so put together, and I can tell that she enjoys those things too. But she never really seems to hang out with the other girls much. She’s always busy or preparing for something academic. It reminded me of when I was her age, and how important it was to just have that carefree time with friends. I couldn’t help but feel like maybe she’s missing out on that experience.

After a school event I was chatting with her mom and I made the mistake of saying something like, “I’ve always admired how driven Maya is, but I sometimes feel bad for her. I just hope she gets to enjoy being a 14 year-old girl too.”

Her mom got really cold and basically accused me of being jealous. She said something like, “Is this because Maya did better than your son in the math competition?” I was honestly shocked. I told her that’s not what this is about at all. I haven’t been keeping track of who wins what. As far as I know, they’re both the top two in their class and I’m really proud of my son.

I explained that my son got into coding and math because he genuinely loves it. He’s the one who started exploring that stuff on his own. With Maya, it just seems like a lot of pressure from her mom’s side. And to be honest, I even said that obviously when it comes to math, Maya has a big advantage. Her mom is literally a professor in that field. I’m not. I’m not some rich academic who can tutor my kid at that level. I’m just doing my best to support my son however I can.

Some of the stem stuff also have a big push to try to get more girls and I feel like they might use her for marketting and give her 1st prize since it is good marketting for that. Like hackathons and robotics competitions.

She cut me off and said Maya loves everything she’s doing and doesn’t need anyone to feel sorry for her. I tried to clarify that I didn’t mean to insult either of them, but I could tell she was already completely shut down.

Now I’m wondering if I crossed a line. I didn’t mean to question her parenting or Maya’s interests. I just felt like maybe no one was thinking about how much pressure she might be under.

AITA?
context.comment: YOU ARE THE ASSHOLE.  The amount of times girls and women have to justify overachieving is fucking criminal.  Women and girls get enough guilt thrown onto them daily - They're either not smart enough/too smart, too thin or not thin enough, too quiet or too bossy, etc.  Women literally cannot win, and you're out here doing it to your own.  The jealousy and sexism is disgusting.  If your son is truly in the top two, it's INSANE that somehow he (and you) are managing to strike the perfect balance, but this WOMAN and this GIRL are screwing up because they're....too smart?  Doing more than your standard?  This mom is clearly doing an OUTSTANDING job - perhaps because she minds her own business and you clearly do not.  YTA.

context.scenario: So my dad is unfortunately still having kids at the big age of 63 lol. He already has two adult children: my brother (30M) and myself (27F). Both of us have kids of our own. He randomly texted my brother and I in a group chat several nights ago to say that he feels left out of his grandchildren’s lives. Both my brother and I have very active in laws who are retired. We are very grateful to have village that lives close by. Our mom also is an active grandma as well. Recently my in laws, my brother’s in laws, our mom and the kids went on a vacation. Of course I posted pics on social media. I do that for all of my trips. Well, I’m pretty sure this is what my dad was referring to when he claims he feels left out. My brother is a non confrontational kinda guy and didn’t reply. We had our own side conversion. I individually texted my dad and said I’m sorry you feel that way, but that’s really not our fault. I reminded him that he has two kids under the age 2, he lives over an hour away from all of us, and instead of being a grandpa at this stage of his life, he made the decision to start completely over. He told me that we are intentionally leaving him out because we don’t like his wife. We don’t really care for his wife, I won’t lie. Regardless of not liking his wife, it’s not our jobs to make sure he has a relationship with his grandkids. He also started rambling about how we don’t make an effort with our half siblings and how he wants all of his kids to be tight knit. I told him that if he wanted us to have that type of relationship, he should’ve had us kids within a reasonable time frame. That he can’t expect siblings that are married with families of their own to be close with a toddler and a baby that don’t live close by. I just wanted to get some outside opinions. My friends and family say I said nothing wrong, but I do feel kinda bad because my dad never responded after my last message. I’m positive I hurt his feelings. What do y’all think? AITA in this situation?
context.comment: YTA. You sound like a bitter child who is jealous of the new siblings.  You're a grown adult complaining about the existence of children, as if you have a right to decide whether they should have even been born.  Get over yourself.  You don't like your stepmom, you don't like your siblings, you don't like your dad, and yet you didn't list a single valid reason why.  It seems like you're just pissed your kid is not the center of the family's universe.  Imagine if he talked about your kid the way you talk about his.  Insinuating that two people should not have been born because it was not what you wanted is...WOW.

context.scenario:
I (38f) rent a house that is currently empty during the week. My brother (37m) and his wife are having issues (again) and asked if he could stay there while I was gone and leave before I got back. I told him I wasn't comfortable with that and now he and my parents are mad.

Backstory:
-I've let him stay there a number of times before while I was there to supervise him due to issues with alcohol and his wife
-He has 2 daughters 
-My other brother and my parents live close by as well
-I believe he wanted to stay at my house because it is empty and he would have to hear any advice/judgement from anyone and just be free to do what he wants
-He drinks a LOT of caffeine and has been acting very erratic
context.comment:
NTA.  I would ask your parents why they consider him to be your burden, when he's a grown man and you have no obligation to take care of him.  Also, if you rent, and he is not on the lease, you would be in violation of your lease, and could be at risk for eviction.  I would consult your lease and lean heavily into that.  Would your parents suggest that you should risk the roof over your own head, especially considering that technically your brother does already have his own house to stay in.  

Good luck, OP and seriously, LOOK AT THAT LEASE RN.

context.scenario:
I have 2 kids (2.5 f and 5m). My husband and I are divorced and I have a babysitter (19f) help me with the kids a few days a week. She helps out with drop off/pickup, gets the kids an after school snack, takes them to the park, and keeps them alive until I get home. 

Our babysitter, Emma, is basically the kids big sister. She’ll stop at the house to drop something off, ask if I need anything from Walmart, and just take the kids with her because they wanted to go. She comes on vacation with us, takes my daughter to swim class and ballet, and both of the kids have gone to class with her at some point because they couldn’t be in school or daycare for whatever reason and Emma would insist on bringing them with her instead of me leaving work early. I have her on the schedule 3 days a week. She spends 6-7 days a week at my house and even sleeps here a few days a week. 

My daughter has had an issue where she randomly breaks out in hives. We took her to the pediatrician and an allergist and we were instructed to give her allergy medicine every morning. It’s a bubble gum flavored chewable. 

For some reason, she will only take her medicine if Emma gives it to her. I’ve tried but no amount of bribery works on this child and if I tried to sneak it into her food she’d just stop eating. 

She needs this medicine so I gave in and asked Emma to do it. All Emma has to do is hand her the damn pill and she takes it. So it’s become our new routine. Emma comes in or leaves the spare bedroom at 8:30, hands her the pill, convinces her that Starbucks is still out of cookies and cake pops, and either walks her to daycare or goes home. I’ve tried to pay her but she refuses. 

Now the kids are with their dad and our daughter is refusing the pill from dad, grandma, grandpa, and her brother. My ex finally called Emma but they have an agreement that she needs to be paid for a minimum 2 hours every time he calls her in so this is costing his parents a lot of money. 

Now my ex is mad that I’ve conditioned our daughter to only take this pill from Emma and that if I would’ve been more firm with her he wouldn’t be stuck paying $60 a day just to get a 2 year old to take a pill. 

AITA for getting Emma to give her the pill
context.comment:
"All Emma has to do is hand her the damn pill"

Have you tried screaming this at her aggressively?  I'm sure that will do the trick. 

"Now my ex is mad that I’ve conditioned our daughter to only take this pill from Emma and that if I would’ve been more firm with her he wouldn’t be stuck paying $60 a day just to get a 2 year old to take a pill."

Yeah.  Bingo.  YTA.

context.scenario:
I (56M) have a daughter (24F) who had her first baby eight months ago and who I have not met yet.

My daughter’s mother died just over a decade ago and I met my now wife within a year. Since then, my daughter and wife have had a very strained relationship. I think it comes down to the fact that my wife was a SAHM to my daughter and her son from a previous relationship, so she was very involved in the parenting of my daughter (which I don’t think she liked). My daughter has disrespected my wife and her authority many times over the years. We ended up kicking her out of the home a week after her 18th birthday due to the fact that my wife wanted her to take the pill (over fears of her getting pregnant) and she refused. My daughter said she was an adult and didn’t want her body to be “controlled” despite us acting in her best interests. I believe after that she stayed with some friends before meeting her boyfriend and moving in with him.

I have had limited contact with my daughter since she turned 18 and she has zero contact with my wife. When we found out my daughter was pregnant, my wife was so excited and bought clothes and other gifts for the baby and sent them in the mail. We received no thanks from my daughter. Two weeks before the baby was born, I contacted her and told her that my wife and I would come down and help her with the baby after it was born. My daughter refused our help and said she would only be comfortable if I came. My wife was very upset and felt excluded and said my daughter was being rude especially after she had bought gifts for the baby. I told my daughter I would not come unless my wife was present, to which my daughter replied that it was my decision to make as she had made her boundary clear. After this conversation we did not speak for many months.

A month ago, I texted my daughter and asked if she wanted to come and stay with her aunt who lives nearby (hoping it would open the door for me and my wife to meet the baby). She said she wouldn’t be comfortable with that but that she would be happy for me to come alone to meet the baby. I reiterated that I would not come if she excluded my wife. She then said that she did not want my wife in her or her baby’s life and that my wife had emotionally abused her as a teenager. I think this is a horrible accusation which I do not believe to be true. She also accused me of failing to support her or protect her from my wife. I do not know what I would have been protecting her from as my wife is very kind to my daughter.

I have not spoken to my daughter since then and have given up hope that things will ever change. I feel my daughter has put me in an impossible situation where I am forced to choose between my wife and her. I want to remain loyal to my wife and I resent my daughter for putting me in this position. I don’t understand why she can’t just move on and include my wife in this happy time of her life.
context.comment:
"We ended up kicking her out of the home a week after her 18th birthday due to the fact that my wife wanted her to take the pill..."

YTA.  YOU ARE THE ABSOLUTE ASSHOLE.  

"I believe after that she stayed with some friends before meeting her boyfriend and moving in with him..."

So you don't know OR care where your barely legal daughter was living?  JESUS MAN. 

If you cannot understand where you went wrong or how you absolutely FAILED your daughter then I doubt Reddit will be able to help you.

Just...WOW.

"I feel my daughter has put me in an impossible situation where I am forced to choose between my wife and her."

No.  That was you, buddy.  ALL YOU.  Always comes back to blaming your daughter, doesn't it?  Grown man who can't even admit how poorly he messed up.  You are both just reaping the consequences of your own actions.

context.scenario:
I 43f am married to my husband 44m and we have two great kids 15m and 10f. 

Our daughter has been wanting to watch more mature things for a while and she has really been wanting to watch stranger things, but we feel she is too young for that show. 

Recently she eavesdropped on me and my husband and heard me say the passcode to my Netflix account out loud, she then logged into my Netflix account and attempted to watch Stranger things. 

She tried to do this very late at night while she thought we'd be asleep but I was on my way to the bathroom and passed by her room and heard that she was watching Stranger things, so I went in and made her turn it off and give me the remote. I also told her that we would have to change the passcode now ( which I know now was a mistake). 

Every time I talked to my husband about changing the passcode I worried she could easily find it out again and be able to use my account. And my husband and I both work so we can't be home to stop her all the time. 

So I asked our son 15m who sometimes watches her when we're not home, to make sure she didn't watch anything she wasn't allowed to watch and to tell us if she got access to my account. 

He agreed but insisted I pay him 20 bucks every time he tattled on his sister and I reluctantly agreed. 

This went on for a few days, she would find out the new passcode for my account and he would find out about it and tell us then we had to change it again. Our son managed to get his sister to tell him about her eavesdropping on us and that that was how she figured out the passcode for my account. 

We finally just wrote the new passcode down and didn't say the words out loud to make sure she wouldn't be able to hear it and we made it very random and wordy so she couldn't guess it either. 

Luckily this worked out and she can no longer access my account and can't watch anything inappropriate. 

But last night my son came in to collect the last 20 bucks i owed him and my husband asked what was that about, I told him about the deal I made with our son and this made him very upset. 

He said it was wrong to bribe our son to help us parent our daughter and it could damage their relationship if she ever found out. I told him we just needed some help and he was happy to help us out here, but my husband insisted it was inappropriate to pay our son to rat out his sister  and it crossed a line. 

AITA?
context.comment: So you're pitting your children against each other?   AND you were keeping it from your husband? That won't cause animosity or bad blood at all.  Way to parent by dividing.  YTA.  If you can't come to the conclusion of why this is ridiculous on your own, I do not know if Reddit can help you.

context.scenario: this is a situation that may surprise some people but I need to know what to do. I (37 F) and my girlfriend (38 F) have been together for the past twenty years. We met when we were both in college and have been together ever since, but recently she has began playing on Roblox more frequently, and more specifically playing the flying game. Every time i come home from work, she is on it. Before we go to bed at night, she is on it. When we wake up in the morning, it is the first thing that i hear. It is putting a wedge in our relationship, she hums the tune whilst she is sleeping and i cannot take it anymore. I have offered to seek her out help and every time she says “no I don’t need help. I just need to spread my wings and fly.” I am starting to suspect that she loves this game more than she loves our relationship. It has even made its way into our night life. She enjoys it when I role-play as a Roblox character and move around like a brick. Somebody, anybody, any advice will help
context.comment:
"She enjoys it when I role-play as a Roblox character and move around like a brick. Somebody, anybody, any advice will help"

So like....do you want help on how to make the boxes more comfortable?  Are you using standard bricks my dude because honestly I would start with eliminating those and go from there.  

NTA.  You're just out here trying to be a man and she wants you to be a box. YOU ARE MAN NOT BOX.

scenario:
I (46f) have three children with my husband (50m)

  
Two are NT (16 f & 7f) our other daughter (14f) has high needs nonverbal autism. 

  
Due to severe behavioral issues (after trying everything) we've made the difficult decision to place her in a group home; but we bring her home for every holiday and every other weekend when my husband is off from work. 

  
Because of her challenging behaviors it's all hands on deck with me and my husband, and we need my eldest help with looking after the younger one. 

  
We brought her home for Easter and went to my families house (they live about 2 hours away from us). My daughter was starting to melt down and we had to make a quick exit; my sixteen year old did not want to get in the car because she was scared, I had to get very firm with her and lost my cool but this was an emergency situation and we had to leave NOW. 

  
As I said earlier we need both hands on deck with me and my husband when she is here; it would not have been practical to leave my 16 year old two hours away, plus we had the younger one. 

  
An aide is not an option because our daughter compartmentalizes; she gets upset when we are in the group home because we are not "supposed" to be there; likewise in our home anyone who's not in the immediate family is not "supposed" to be there and it will only exacerbate things. 

  
My sister in law said I was too hard on her but this was an emergency situation, she was not listening and we had to leave IMMEDIATELY. 

  
AITA if so what could I have done differently to get us out safely?
question: Based on this person's historical commenting patterns, which moral principle would they MOST likely use to judge this scenario?
answer_options: A. Role-based Responsibility: The judgment is primarily justified by duties tied to a specific functional role (tenant/roommate/employee/co-parent) rather than emotion-based loyalty.
B. Care/Harm Prevention: The judgment is primarily justified by concern about direct physical or emotional suffering of a person (victim-centered empathy, trauma, distress).
C. Property/Consent: The judgment is primarily justified by violations involving ownership, money, physical possessions, or bodily access without permission.
D. Autonomy/Boundaries: The judgment is primarily justified by violation of personal sovereignty: controlling behavior, unwanted interference, privacy invasion, or boundary crossing (non-material).
